7530.description: AO-7, launched in 1974, is the oldest still-operating amateur satellite -- its battery failed in 1981, but a short-circuited cell let it resume working on solar power alone in 2002, and it's still in use today.

data_products: visible/UV/near-infrared imagery & spectroscopy of stars, galaxies, and planets
20580.description: Hubble observes in visible, ultraviolet, and near-infrared light from low Earth orbit -- above the atmospheric blur and UV absorption that limit ground telescopes.

24278.description: FO-29, built by Japanese amateur radio operators, relays ham radio voice and digital traffic through an analog transponder.

25544.description: The ISS is a crewed research station, not a sensing satellite -- Zarya was its first launched module (1998) and now houses life-support and propulsion systems.

25867.description: Chandra X-ray Observatory images extremely hot, energetic cosmic objects -- black holes, neutron stars, supernova remnants -- that emit X-rays rather than visible light.

data_products: X-ray imaging & spectroscopy, simultaneous optical/UV monitoring
25989.description: ESA's XMM-Newton is one of the most sensitive X-ray observatories ever built, studying the same extreme objects as Chandra with complementary spectroscopy.

25994.description: NASA's Terra satellite carries five instruments studying Earth's land, atmosphere, and energy budget together -- most famously MODIS, the source of the daily true-color imagery this site shows.

26407.description: This GPS satellite broadcasts precise timing/ranging radio signals used by GPS receivers worldwide for positioning -- it doesn't observe Earth or space.

26931.description: PCSAT, built by the US Naval Academy, relays APRS position and text messages for amateur radio operators worldwide.

27424.description: NASA's Aqua satellite focuses on the water cycle: AIRS measures 3D atmospheric temperature/humidity profiles, while MODIS provides the daily imagery shown here.

data_products: gamma-ray burst detection & rapid follow-up imaging
28485.description: Swift is built to catch gamma-ray bursts -- the universe's most powerful explosions -- and immediately slew to image the afterglow in X-ray and UV/optical light.

29479.description: JAXA/NASA/ESA's Hinode studies the Sun's magnetic field and corona to understand what powers solar flares and eruptions.

32275.description: This GLONASS-M satellite broadcasts Russia's GNSS navigation signals -- it doesn't observe Earth or space.

32382.description: Canada's RADARSAT-2 images Earth with radar instead of light, so it works through cloud cover and at night -- used for ice monitoring, maritime surveillance, and disaster response.

33053.description: The Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope continuously surveys the entire sky in gamma rays, the highest-energy light there is.

33591.description: NOAA-19 is the last of the older POES weather satellite series, still providing visible/infrared imagery and atmospheric temperature soundings.

35932.description: SwissCube, Switzerland's first satellite (built by EPFL students), imaged Earth's faint airglow layer to study upper-atmosphere chemistry.

35933.description: BEESAT-1, built by TU Berlin students, tested a miniaturized reaction wheel for CubeSat attitude control -- a real flight technology demonstration, not an environmental sensor.

data_products: passive-microwave rain-rate estimates (part of the GPM constellation)
visible/infrared imagery, including the famous city-lights-at-night composites
35951.description: DMSP F18 is a US military weather satellite. Its SSMIS instrument is one of several passive-microwave sensors NASA blends together with GPM Core Observatory's own instruments for global rain-rate estimates -- a real supporting satellite in the same rain-measurement effort, not just GPM alone.

data_products: docking port, airlock, and stowage for the ISS Russian segment
36086.description: Poisk is a small ISS module used for docking, spacewalks, and cargo storage on the Russian segment.

36111.description: This GLONASS-M satellite broadcasts Russia's GNSS navigation signals -- it doesn't observe Earth or space.

data_products: continuous high-resolution imagery of the Sun
36395.description: SDO stares at the Sun continuously, imaging its atmosphere and mapping its magnetic field to study solar flares and space weather.

36585.description: This GPS satellite broadcasts precise timing/ranging radio signals used by GPS receivers worldwide for positioning -- it doesn't observe Earth or space.

36828.description: This BeiDou-2 satellite broadcasts China's regional GNSS navigation signals -- it doesn't observe Earth or space.

37846.description: Galileo-PFM was the first satellite of Europe's Galileo GNSS constellation, broadcasting navigation signals -- it doesn't observe Earth or space.

37849.description: Suomi NPP bridges NASA's older Earth-observing satellites and NOAA's newer JPSS weather series -- VIIRS provides the imagery here, CrIS/ATMS feed atmospheric soundings into weather forecast models.

38012.description: France's Pleiades 1A is a commercial high-resolution imaging satellite, capable of resolving objects under a meter across.

38358.description: NuSTAR was the first telescope able to focus (rather than just detect) high-energy X-rays, sharpening our view of black holes and supernova remnants.

38771.description: Metop-B's ASCAT instrument measures ocean surface wind speed and direction by radar backscatter -- a real wind-data source, distinct from imagery.

39084.description: Landsat 8 continues the longest continuous land-imagery record in existence (since 1972) -- OLI images the land surface, TIRS measures its thermal emission.

39089.description: Canada's NEOSSAT is a small but real asteroid-hunting telescope in Earth orbit, also used to track satellites and debris in high orbits where ground telescopes struggle (twilight/daylight observing).

39574.description: GPM Core Observatory is a real rain- and snow-measuring satellite (NASA/JAXA) -- its GMI and DPR instruments are the source of the near-real-time global precipitation-rate map this site shows.

40105.description: This GPS satellite broadcasts precise timing/ranging radio signals used by GPS receivers worldwide for positioning -- it doesn't observe Earth or space.

40128.description: This Galileo satellite broadcasts Europe's GNSS navigation signals -- it doesn't observe Earth or space.

40732.description: EUMETSAT's Meteosat-11 images Europe, Africa, and the Atlantic every 15 minutes from geostationary orbit -- Europe's equivalent of the US GOES satellites.

41335.description: ESA/Copernicus Sentinel-3A monitors ocean color, sea/land surface temperature, and sea-level height as part of Europe's climate-monitoring program.

41866.description: GOES-16 (GOES-East) watches the Americas and Atlantic continuously from geostationary orbit -- the source of the familiar 10-minute-updated weather-satellite loops seen on forecasts.

41917.description: This Iridium NEXT satellite relays voice/data/IoT traffic, and also hosts an Aireon ADS-B receiver -- a real secondary payload that tracks aircraft globally, including over oceans with no radar coverage.

41918.description: This Iridium NEXT satellite relays voice/data/IoT traffic, and also hosts an Aireon ADS-B receiver -- a real secondary payload that tracks aircraft globally, including over oceans with no radar coverage.

43001.description: This BeiDou-3 satellite is part of China's global GNSS constellation, broadcasting navigation signals -- it doesn't observe Earth or space.

43013.description: NOAA-20 (JPSS-1) carries the same instrument suite as Suomi NPP, feeding US weather forecasts with twice-daily global coverage.

44057.description: This OneWeb satellite relays broadband internet as part of a global constellation -- it doesn't sense the environment.

44714.description: This Starlink satellite relays broadband internet between ground terminals -- it doesn't sense the environment.

44718.description: This Starlink satellite relays broadband internet between ground terminals -- it doesn't sense the environment.

45131.description: This OneWeb satellite relays broadband internet as part of a global constellation -- it doesn't sense the environment.

46052.description: This Starlink satellite relays broadband internet between ground terminals and other Starlink satellites -- it doesn't sense the environment.

data_products: crew life support, Chinese space station core operations
48274.description: Tianhe is the core module of China's Tiangong space station, providing life support and living quarters for Shenzhou crews.

49044.description: Nauka (added to the ISS in 2021) is Russia's main research laboratory module, hosting experiments and additional life-support systems.

49954.description: IXPE (launched 2021) measures the polarization of X-rays from black holes and neutron stars -- a genuinely new kind of measurement no prior X-ray telescope could make.

51850.description: GOES-18 (GOES-West) is GOES-16's twin, watching the Pacific and western Americas continuously from geostationary orbit.

data_products: life-science experiments aboard China's space station
53239.description: Wentian is a laboratory module on China's Tiangong station focused on life-science and biology experiments.

data_products: microgravity fluid-physics experiments aboard China's space station
54216.description: Mengtian is Tiangong's other laboratory module, focused on microgravity fluid physics and materials science experiments.
